tasks.title: W04 API: Establish route, handler, and OpenAPI drift baseline
tasks.prompt: Create the first feedback slice for the API sweep by inventorying implemented backend routes against the current OpenAPI contract. Treat `backend/server/server.go`, touched handlers, DTOs, and handler tests as the source of truth, then compare them with `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` and `backend/docs/API-ISSUES.md`. Record mismatches by route, method, request shape, response shape, status code, auth requirement, and error/reason behavior. Fix only narrow obvious documentation drift needed to make the baseline truthful; do not redesign routes or response envelopes in this task.
acceptance_criteria: Implemented routes are inventoried against `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` with mismatches grouped by endpoint area.
The baseline identifies auth, request, response, status-code, content-type, and error/reason drift without inventing future API behavior.
Any narrow OpenAPI edits made in this task reflect implemented behavior and keep Swagger rendering valid.

focus: source-of-truth ordering from server.go to handlers/DTOs to OpenAPI

notes: Use `software-planner-agent` posture: this is the first useful feedback slice, not a broad rewrite.
Use `software-action-openapi-contract-agent` and API conformance skills for route/spec comparison.
Source-of-truth order: `server.go`, handlers/DTOs/tests, `docs/openapi.yaml`, then `API-ISSUES.md`.

tasks.title: W04 API: Define stable reason vocabulary and response-boundary helpers
tasks.prompt: Prepare the API/auth contract cleanup by identifying the stable public `reason` vocabulary for validation, authorization, authentication, password-change gating, not-found, and business-rule failures. Reuse existing response helpers where practical and add narrow boundary helpers only where they remove ad hoc raw error strings from HTTP handlers. Keep business logic in domain services and keep handlers as transport mappers. Update focused tests for the chosen helper behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A stable initial `reason` vocabulary exists for the API sweep's common failure classes.
Touched handlers can map policy outcomes to sanitized route-visible reasons without leaking internal error strings.
Focused tests cover the helper behavior and at least one handler-level mapping.

focus: no domain policy moved into HTTP response helpers

out_of_scope: forcing every endpoint into a universal envelope in this task
changing domain error semantics unrelated to API mapping
notes: This task answers the design-plan W04 open question about stable `reason` vocabulary.
Apply envelope direction only where the current route behavior can be safely tightened without a flag-day API migration.
Preserve public/private model boundaries in API responses.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align auth, password-change, setup, and bootstrap-visible routes
tasks.prompt: Sweep the auth-adjacent and setup/bootstrap-visible API surface after the runtime/config service work. Verify and, where needed, fix the implemented behavior and OpenAPI contract for login, change-password, must-change-password enforcement, setup/frontend setup, health/docs exposure, and admin add-user behavior. Preserve the current login token contract while documenting and testing server-side password-change gating for authenticated actions.
acceptance_criteria: Login and change-password routes match the implemented contract in code, tests, and OpenAPI.
Must-change-password users are allowed only through intended flows and blocked from other authenticated actions through server-side enforcement.
Setup and frontend setup routes remain service-backed and their OpenAPI schemas match the actual payloads.

focus: OpenAPI updates for touched auth/setup paths

notes: Design decision DP-006 is binding: enforce must-change-password restrictions without redesigning token issuance.
Use `software-action-architecture-agent` for auth boundary review if handler/domain ownership becomes unclear.
Keep `/v0/setup` and `/v0/setup/frontend` route semantics stable.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align markets routes, business outcomes, and Swagger schemas
tasks.prompt: Sweep the markets API surface end to end: create, legacy create alias, list, status filtering, search, details, projected probability, market positions, market bets, and resolution. Verify that handlers stay thin around domain services, business-rule outcomes map to stable public reasons, and `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` documents the implemented request/response/status behavior. Add or update focused handler/domain contract tests where drift is found.
acceptance_criteria: Markets route request and response schemas in OpenAPI match implemented DTOs and response helpers.
Market business-rule failures use stable sanitized reasons at the HTTP boundary.
Handler tests or focused API tests cover at least the highest-risk create, search/list, detail, and resolve flows touched by the sweep.

focus: thin handler to domain service boundary

out_of_scope: moving market policy back into handlers
notes: Preserve integer-first accounting and existing market-economics behavior.
Keep repository-owned legacy model translation intact; do not leak persistence models into route DTOs.
Treat `POST /v0/create` as a legacy alias if still implemented.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align bets buy/sell routes and market-bets contract
tasks.prompt: Sweep the bets API surface for buy/place, sell, user betting constraints, market bet history, and related math-backed response behavior. Preserve bets terminology as the current public contract. Align business-rule failures such as insufficient balance, invalid outcome, market closed, no position, and dust-cap outcomes with stable public reasons and OpenAPI schemas. Add or update focused API and domain tests where contract drift is found.
acceptance_criteria: Bet buy/sell and market-bets OpenAPI operations match implemented requests, responses, auth, and status codes.
Business-rule outcomes from the bets domain map to stable route-visible reasons without raw internal leakage.
Focused tests cover successful buy/sell plus representative failure cases that clients depend on.

focus: market math and accounting behavior preservation

notes: Preserve 32-bit safety guards when converting `uint64` to platform-sized `uint`.
Keep time-based market enforcement on the server side.
Do not rename tags, paths, or schemas from bets to trades in this wave.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align users, positions, portfolio, credit, and financial routes
tasks.prompt: Sweep user-facing authenticated and public API routes, including profile updates, public/private profile payloads, personal links, emoji/display metadata, user positions, portfolio, credit, financial summaries, user markets, and transaction-visible behavior. Preserve public versus private response boundaries, must-change-password enforcement, and domain-owned user business rules. Update OpenAPI and focused tests to match implemented behavior.
acceptance_criteria: User, position, portfolio, credit, and financial route schemas match actual public/private DTO boundaries.
Authenticated user routes enforce password-change gating consistently except for intended exempt flows.
Focused tests cover representative public profile, private profile, mutation, position, and financial/credit responses.

focus: user route auth and password-change coverage
OpenAPI schema parity for touched user-facing endpoints

notes: Public versus private model boundaries are a hard invariant.
Known historical handlers/users failures should be rechecked and classified instead of hidden.
Keep transaction/accounting behavior integer-first and domain-owned.

tasks.title: W04 API: Align analytics, stats, metrics, leaderboard, and CMS/home routes
tasks.prompt: Sweep the remaining reporting and content-facing backend routes: stats, system metrics, global and market leaderboards, analytics-backed positions where not covered by user routes, CMS/homepage content, and root/home handlers. Verify that these routes use current response helpers, service-backed configuration where applicable, and OpenAPI schemas that match implemented payloads. Add or update focused tests around response shape and error behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Stats, metrics, leaderboard, CMS, and home route OpenAPI entries match implemented behavior.
Config-derived reporting routes continue to use the configuration service boundary.
Focused tests or conformance checks cover representative success and failure responses.
out_of_scope: universal response-envelope migration outside touched routes
notes: This is the stop-and-review point for route coverage before global conformance work.
Do not revive stale historical API docs as source of truth when current code differs.

tasks.title: W04 API: Add deterministic OpenAPI and API conformance regression checks
tasks.prompt: After endpoint-area sweeps are complete, add or tighten deterministic API conformance checks that prevent route/spec drift from returning. Use kin-openapi validation, the existing OpenAPI test, targeted route/spec sync checks, and focused handler/API tests. Where practical, add helper scripts or tests that compare route inventory and OpenAPI operations without requiring a full live environment. Keep checks scoped and deterministic.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAPI structural validation passes for `backend/docs/openapi.yaml` and embedded Swagger assets remain valid.
Deterministic checks catch missing or stale OpenAPI operations for the swept route areas.
Focused tests for touched handlers and API helpers run reliably without requiring external secrets or non-local services.
notes: Run the OpenAPI navigation and kin-openapi skills before any runtime probing.
If broader Go tests fail for unrelated existing issues, classify them with exact package/test names.
Prefer small regression checks that future API tasks can run cheaply.

tasks.title: W04 API: Run runtime API conformance, reconcile Swagger docs, and verify sweep completion
tasks.prompt: Perform the final API sweep validation. Start or use a local non-Docker backend runtime when available, run scoped Schemathesis/runtime conformance after deterministic OpenAPI checks pass, verify Swagger UI serves the updated contract, and reconcile `backend/docs/API-ISSUES.md` so it lists only genuinely deferred decisions. Use the verifier agent for diff sanity checks and final validation evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime API conformance is run against a local backend or the exact blocker is documented with all deterministic checks completed.
Swagger/OpenAPI artifacts parse, validate, embed, and serve correctly for the updated contract.
`API-ISSUES.md` reflects current implemented behavior and preserves only real deferred decisions such as limited-scope token redesign, public route reorganization, and bets-to-trades rename.

focus: runtime conformance evidence or exact runtime blocker
diff sanity and architecture invariant preservation

notes: Use `socialpredict-local-backend-runtime` before Schemathesis if no backend is already running.
Keep final docs grounded in implemented behavior, not aspirational redesign notes.
This is the final stop-and-review point for the API sweep queue.
